Nitpick and correction - *Honey is NOT classified as ''flammable''*

A material is considered flammable if it has a flash point of any temperature below 37.8 ºC (100F). A material is considered combustible if it has a flash point higher than 37.8 ºC and below 93.3 ºC (200F)

Most types of honey can burn around 93ºC (199F) - so it is just under the threshold of ''Combustible'
